extraction.article_title: Hackers Exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign

ttps.description: Exploit public-facing application vulnerability in vulnerable Next.js applications to gain initial access.
Harvest sensitive data from local files, environment variables, and application secrets after compromise.
Search for and collect credentials, API keys, SSH private keys, cloud tokens, and other secrets stored on disk or in environment variables.

attack_chain: Automated scanning for vulnerable public-facing Next.js applications
Deployment of a temporary-directory script to enumerate secrets
Extraction of environment variables, database credentials, SSH keys, API keys, and cloud tokens
Exfiltration of harvested credentials to attacker-controlled infrastructure

proposed_vectors.name_ko: 공개 Next.js 원격 코드 실행 / 비밀정보 수집

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A remotely reachable flaw in a Next.js application is used to execute attacker-controlled code, which then enumerates local environment variables, config files, and key material for exfiltration.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload: bash -lc 'echo START; env | sort | sed -n "1,40p"; [ -f .env ] && cat .env; [ -f /proc/self/environ ] && tr "\0" "\n" < /proc/self/environ | sed -n "1,40p"; echo END'
detect: Response includes environment variable names such as AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, DATABASE_URL, GITHUB_TOKEN, or SSH_AUTH_SOCK
Unexpected command output or shell errors in application response

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the vulnerable app can be induced to fetch attacker-supplied URLs, the request can target cloud metadata services or internal endpoints to retrieve instance credentials or internal secrets.
detect: Response includes an IAM role name or credential JSON
Application server makes outbound request to link-local metadata IP
Unexpected metadata-related strings such as AccessKeyId or Token

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A compromised web app or script can read files outside the intended directory by manipulating path inputs, enabling access to configuration files and private keys.
detect: Error messages indicate successful file read or path resolution outside the document root
Private file contents such as PEM headers or .env values appear in the response

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If harvested credentials grant access to internal panels or object storage, predictable identifiers may allow unauthorized retrieval of additional secrets or assets.
detect: Response returns another user's secret or object
Status 200 on an identifier that should be unauthorized
Different IDs return sensitive records without access control enforcement
